title: Binary search on a sorted integer slice

informal_statement.text:
Given a sorted slice of integers in nondecreasing order and a target value, return Some(i) where arr[i] == target, or None if the target is absent. If multiple indices match, returning any valid index is acceptable.

Benchmark note (arrays_binary_search_004): Variant 4 stresses witness existence vs uniqueness and forbids smuggling extra outputs.
Authoring lens: Lens 4 targets disconnected postconditions that omit necessary quantifier structure.
preconditions: arr is sorted in nondecreasing order
required_properties: if the function returns Some(i), then i < arr.len() and arr[i] == target
if the function returns None, then target does not occur in arr
the function terminates for every input
edge_cases: single-element slice not containing the target
